compiled_state.title: T3: add --output to lifecycle/result commands (create/claim/transition/reopen/delete)
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description: Add structured --output json to the lifecycle commands so callers stop id-scraping. create -> {id,alias,title}; claim -> {ticket_id,status,assignee}; transition/reopen -> {ticket_id,from,to,newly_unblocked[]}; delete -> {ticket_id,deleted,newly_unblocked[]}. Preserve exit codes (esp. 10). Reuse the centralized output helper from T2.

comments.body:
TARGETS (derived; to be confirmed). Story = add `--output json` to lifecycle/result commands. Depends on T2 (reuses ticket-output.sh).

ENGINE:
- ticket-create.sh — add --output json -> {id,alias,title}; default human line + bare id unchanged.
- ticket-claim.sh — --output json -> {ticket_id,status,assignee}; preserve exit 10.
- ticket-lifecycle.sh (transition + reopen) — --output json -> {ticket_id,from,to,newly_unblocked[]}; preserve exit 10.
- delete path (find: ticket-delete*?; delete is in dispatcher) — --output json -> {ticket_id,deleted,newly_unblocked[]}.
- rebar dispatcher: usage strings for create/claim/transition/reopen/delete.

LIBRARY (src/rebar/__init__.py): create_ticket/claim/transition/reopen already synthesize dicts client-side — reconcile shapes with engine --output json (single source). delete wrapper (add if missing).
MCP (mcp_server.py): create_ticket return {id,alias} (Mismatch C); transition/claim/reopen StatusResultOut.
TESTS: tests/interfaces/test_ws5e_ergonomics.py (create returns id+alias), parity tests; new lifecycle --output tests.
SCHEMAS (coordinate w/ T1): create_result, status_result, transition_result, delete_result.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
TARGETS — VERIFICATION CORRECTIONS (Explore audit). MATERIAL routing fixes:
* There is NO ticket-lifecycle.sh and NO ticket-delete.sh. Correct routing:
  - create -> ticket_create() in ticket-lib-api.sh (canonical in-process path; standalone ticket-create.sh also emits the "Created ...\n<id>" lines — both paths print, handle both).
  - claim  -> ticket-claim.sh (direct exec; CLAIMED: line at ~:79; exit 10 at ~:72).
  - transition -> ticket_transition() in ticket-lib-api.sh (wraps ticket-transition.sh; UNBLOCKED: line; exit 10 at ticket-transition.sh:397).
  - reopen -> ticket_transition() hardcoded closed->open (same path as transition).
  - delete -> ticket_delete() in ticket-lib-api.sh:2041 (Deleted...\nUNBLOCKED: ; --user-approved guard, no exit 10).
* All proposed JSON shapes are FEASIBLE from data each command already has.
* SCOPE CLARIFICATION: delete has NO library wrapper and NO MCP tool today, and is intentionally NOT in the MCP write-tool set (see CLAUDE.md). => delete --output json is CLI/ENGINE-ONLY; do NOT add delete to lib/MCP in this story.
* claim()/transition() in __init__.py SYNTHESIZE their result dicts client-side (hardcoded). After engine gains --output json, reconcile so the library PARSES engine JSON (single source) rather than re-deriving — keeps shapes from drifting.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: DONE (commit pending). --output json on create/claim/transition/reopen/delete with the epic-spec shapes; close-path compact stdout leak fixed. Library now parses engine --output json (single source); create drops the extra show() call. MCP create->{id,alias} (mismatch C), claim typed; transition/reopen stay dict (from= keyword). 4 lifecycle schemas + conformance + probe section. delete stays CLI-only by design. Full suite 1293 passed; probe 134 passed.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
